pi_items.source: Task description states: 'Create a new SLD style in GeoServer named 'blue_polygon''. This value is consistently referenced in export_result.sh (line 13) and verifier.py (line 17).

Task description states: 'solid blue fill (#0000FF)'. Verified against SLD 1.0.0 specification and GeoServer 2.25.2 documentation. #0000FF is the standard hexadecimal representation of pure blue (RGB 0, 0, 255). This value is checked in export_result.sh (lines 46, 57, 152) and verifier.py (line 68).

Task description states: 'dark blue outline (#000080, 1 pixel width)'. Verified against SLD 1.0.0 specification and GeoServer 2.25.2 documentation. #000080 is the standard hexadecimal representation of dark blue/navy (RGB 0, 0, 128). This value is checked in export_result.sh (lines 48, 63, 153) and verifier.py (line 73).

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified. The task requires creating an SLD style named 'blue_polygon' in GeoServer 2.25.2 with a solid blue fill color (#0000FF, RGB 0,0,255) and a dark blue stroke color (#000080, RGB 0,0,128). These hexadecimal color values follow the standard SLD 1.0.0 specification for color parameters. The verification scripts check for these exact color values in the generated SLD content.
